data.epi_content:
I had previously read a rather impressive essay while in a dirt pit.

I believe it was titled "The Myth of Sisyphus."

Reading with a serious mind isn't exactly a pleasant experience, so I didn't particularly enjoy it, but at that time, I had nothing else to do besides reading.

Though the content itself was simple yet complex, and seemed to drag dreams down to reality, leaving me with a somewhat heavy feeling, what could I do? That was the only book I had with me then.

I could go on at length about my thoughts on that collection of essays, or essay if you will, but I'm not particularly in the mood for that right now.

And I have no friends to share it with anyway.

In any case, there was something that annoying, lanky Frenchman who wrote the essay once said.

"Suicide is a confession that life is not worth living." Or something like that.

It feels like I'm fitting his words into my situation in a completely different way than he intended, but strangely.

Really strangely. Such thoughts keep swirling in my head.

People are creatures who interpret what they hear in ways that suit them best.

Perhaps now is the time for me to confess that life is not worth living.

Or maybe this is just a thought that comes from momentary depression, and if I actually tried to die, I'd be too scared and run away.

It's utterly absurd.

...Of course, the sentences I just listed are just nonsense. Extravagant, false, meaningless, useless drivel.

But life is also like that.

At least for me right now, it's fundamentally absurd and meaningless.

With such idle thoughts and completely drained of energy, I remained sitting for quite some time.

Because I had neither the thought nor the strength to get up.

Lunch break was two hours, wasn't it?

If I sit here for about an hour, wallowing in my misery, perhaps I'll be able to stand properly again.

Come to think of it, maybe I should have screamed.

Then someone might have casually used healing magic on me, like giving alms.

Though tears trickled down slightly, I couldn't scream or anything.

As that girl said, I was born this way and raised this way.

After some time passed, my legs seemed to tremble less.

I pressed my hands against the ground to stand up, put strength in my legs and got halfway up...

"Eek."

With a thud, my strength gave out.

Stepping backward to avoid falling, I bumped slightly against the wall, and ended up standing while leaning against it.

I slowly let the strength drain from my legs and decided to rest a bit more with my back against the wall.

Perhaps because the floor is cold, my whole body shivers.

My nose feels slightly blocked and I feel a bit feverish, like I'm about to catch a cold.

Sure enough, a tickling sensation begins in my nose.

Life is painful.

As soon as that thought crosses my mind, the tears I had been holding back start to burst out.

"What...hiccup...did I do wrong?"

Even as I spoke, my throat choked up as the burned area started to hurt.

I think I just started crying out of sadness.

Actually, I had been trying to appear calm while holding back the dam of emotions that was about to collapse, but when something like this happens, I can't help but cry.

Right? It should be fine since no one's watching.

"..hiccup."

Once I start crying, it's not just the sad emotions but the mental exertion from crying that makes my head feel like it's about to split.

Especially like someone sawing along a line running from the left side of my head to the back of my skull.

After that, my eyes hurt like they're about to pop out.
data.epi_content2:
From that moment on, there are no more crying sounds, just occasional hiccups and meaningless tears dripping down.

In the end, even crying out loud is something that requires a healthy body and energy.

It seemed that even the simple act of crying properly was difficult for this pathetic young lady.

There was no one around watching me.

I wiped the tears from my eyes, which must have turned red, with my sleeve, and walked unsteadily toward the exit of the classroom, leaning against the wall.

Then I collapsed again from weakness.

I wonder if I should exercise to build up my stamina, but where would I do that?

Should I quit the Academy?

If I just lock myself in my room and don't attend classes for a few months, they'll kick me out anyway.

But then, being a local noble, I'd have to stay in the Capital for at least five years.

Three years and several months remain.

There's no flexibility.

Then I'd wander the streets of the Capital, get kidnapped in some back alley and sold as a slave to someone, or walk into a brothel myself to sell my body, or what else?

No one would hire a young woman who is inexperienced, sickly, and merely young rather than educated.

Because I'm useless.

What's keeping me uselessly tied to the Capital... no, that's not it. It's only useless to me.

It's terribly useful.

Whether a family has fallen, has no money, might die, or conversely is extremely successful, forcing noble children to stay in the Capital demonstrates power.

Ah, long live the great Emperor.

May you truly rattle your dentures until you die.

.

.

.

.

.

First, I get up from my seat.

And to ignore the fatigue and slight pain in my body, I continued with various thoughts in my head.

After a villain falls and exits, no spotlight is shone on their miserable life unless there's an intention to mock it.

It's just wrapped up later with a brief line like "made an extreme choice within the Academy."

And such deaths usually end with a bit of pity and mockery rather than sadness.

Whether from readers or people living in this world.

To them, my death is less sad than Vivian scraping her knee.

So is everything already decided?

Then why was I stuffed into this body?

Is it the feeling that if you do bad things, you'll be punished?

And who am I talking to?

Rather than talking to someone, I'm just grumbling.

No one is listening, and my throat hurts too much to speak aloud, but just thinking about it relieves stress.

Anyway, rather than falling into a strange world and spreading my legs in front of rich pig bastards, dying seems like a definitely better choice.

"Hee."

A hollow laugh escapes me.

And since prostitution involves selling not just the body but also laughter, if even this laughter shed from a bit of misery were to be priced...

Ah, this won't do.

No pain, no fear.

I need to find a new method.

Now I leave the empty classroom and walk out to where people are.
data.epi_content3:
After exiting the door, I walked for quite some time down the corridor where some children were chatting.

Keeping my back as straight as possible, maintaining a dignified gait.

This terrible habit ingrained in my body maintained a dignified appearance whenever I thought others were watching, no matter how much pain I was in.

As if the girl who had just been sitting on the floor whining in pain had disappeared somewhere, I looked somewhat in pain but gave off the impression of a high-born lady to anyone who saw me.

Though it felt burdensome even to breathe, I went down the stairs past the corridor.

It's only a bit sad that receiving envious, mocking, contemptuous, disdainful, yet sympathetic gazes has become a familiar thing now.

While walking toward the dormitory building, encountering more students' gazes and whispers, someone blocked my path.

Long pants, a top that looked like a formal suit, a solid-looking body—probably a man.

I've heard stories of girls who wear men's uniforms as a hobby, but they wouldn't have such flat chests, at least.

I'm short, so I can't see who it is unless I look up.

But just maintaining proper posture and taking normal steps was already my limit.

I tried to walk around to the side, hoping it was just a mischievous prank, and tried to move away, but the person blocking my path wouldn't budge at all.

"Erica."

In a slightly mumbling voice, I heard what sounded like my name being called.

Perhaps because I'm tired, all the sounds around me seem slightly blurred.

"Move... please."

Just uttering those two syllables without moving my mouth much was painfully difficult.

Unfortunately, the creator of this world didn't seem to think of giving me healing magic, which is just regrettable.

Other people could just ask a talented friend who's learning healing magic, but I don't have anything like that.

But there should be an infirmary to go to when injured, right?

When would I ever find it? I feel like I'll collapse and die just walking to the dormitory.

Why won't this person in front of me move aside in the first place?

I don't think I've wronged any man.

Who knows, maybe Lydia sent him.

Encouraging him by saying that now that I have no backing or ability, there won't be any trouble even if they mess with me.

Is it those people who came at night to check if the door was closed?

If it had been open, what would have happened?

Maybe nothing would have happened.

Because I was holding a black pistol with gold decorations in my trembling hands, ready to shoot.

But now I don't have anything in my hands that might bring peace to my mind, though I'm not sure if I could use it.

I just can't live positively.

I can't even move past one person in front of me.

I'm just an ordinary person who breaks down when faced with a few misfortunes.

I am not Michel Petrucciani.

Ah, this is hard.

I just feel too exhausted.

"I think I spoke too harshly last time, so I'm sor... huh? Erica?"

My vision gradually blurs, and I start to feel slightly nauseous.

"Ugh..."

I collapse slightly as my strength gives out.

Squinting my eyes to try to move forward, I see Evan in front of me.

What is this, was I sleeping in the dormitory?

I guess it was just a dream.
